trials.title: Timing of CHolecystectomy In Severe PAncreatitis

criteria.text: Age ≥18 years
Diagnosis of pancreatitis according to Atlanta guidelines, moderately severe or severe pancreatitis (APACHE score ≥8 on admittance)
criteria.evidence: severe epigastric pain radiating to back, nausea, vomiting, elevated lipase/amylase

criteria.text: Biliary pancreatitis diagnosed on imaging (be it ultrasound, magnetic resonance imaging and/or tomography)
Recovery of pancreatitis by tolerance of oral intake (defined as 24 hours of food consumption of any consistency without emetic episodes and pain defined as 4/10 on the visual analogue score of pain)
criteria.reasoning: No info on oral intake recovery

criteria.text: Pregnancy
History of cholecystectomy
Chronic pancreatitis
criteria.reasoning: Chronic alcohol use may imply chronic pancreatitis

criteria.text: Active malignant disease
Septic shock

trials.rationale: Severe epigastric pain, elevated lipase/amylase, no chronic pancreatitis

trials.title: Diabetes RElated to Acute Pancreatitis and Its Mechanisms: Metabolic Outcomes Using Novel CGM Metrics

criteria.text: Diagnosis of acute pancreatitis (AP) 0-90 days prior to enrollment
criteria.evidence: severe epigastric pain radiating to back, nausea, vomiting, elevated lipase/amylase
criteria.reasoning: Symptoms suggest AP, but no diagnosis

criteria.text: Participant fully understands and is able to participate in all aspects of the study, including providing informed consent, completion of case report forms, telephone interviews, metabolic testing, and planned longitudinal follow-ups

Diagnosis of definite chronic pancreatitis (CP) at enrollment based on either of the following criteria met by computed tomography (CT) scan or Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) or Magnetic Resonance Cholangiopancreatography (MRCP): (a) Parenchymal or ductal calcifications on CT scan; (b) Intraductal filling defects suggestive of calcifications on MRI and/or MRCP
Potential participants with post-endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) AP who are hospitalized for <48 hours
Prior direct endoscopic necrosectomy of the pancreas or percutaneous necrosectomy or drainage of necrotic collection(s) before enrollment
Pancreatic tumors, including ductal adenocarcinoma, neuroendocrine tumors, and metastasis
Confirmed or suspected cystic tumor associated with main pancreatic duct dilation, or believed to be the cause of AP

Severe systemic illness that in the judgement of the investigative team will confound outcome assessments of diabetes mellitus and immunological outcomes or pose additional risk for harms, including history of solid organ transplant, acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS), active treatment for cancer within 12 months prior to enrollment, chronic kidney disease with estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) < 30 or on dialysis prior to AP

trials.rationale: Acute pancreatitis possible, but many unknowns

trials.title: Registry of Patients Undergoing Endoscopic Management of Pancreatic Fluid Collections

criteria.text: Age ≥ 18 years
All patients undergoing endoscopic treatment of pancreatic fluid collections
Age < 18 years
Patients who did not receive endoscopic treatment of pancreatic fluid collections

trials.rationale: No clear indication of endoscopic treatment of pancreatic fluid collections

criteria.text: Any patient undergoing a Pancreaticoduodenectomy in one of the four participating centers for a benign or malignant tumor of the pancreatic head
Age < 18 years
Pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding women
Indication other than tumor-related (e.g., chronic pancreatitis)
Patient with an infectious syndrome at the time of inclusion
Preoperative immunosuppression
Patient with HIV (and CD4 < cells/mm³)
Immunosuppressive treatment other than chemotherapy : Biotherapy, Corticosteroid therapy >10 mg/day or cumulative dose >700 mg prednisolone equivalent

questions.question: What are your known medical conditions?
Are you taking any immunosuppressive medications?
What is your current treatment plan and hospitalization status?

trials.title: Effects of Different Treatment Schemes on the Regulation and Recurrence of Graves' Disease

criteria.text: Graves hyperthyroidism (responsible for doctors' clinical diagnosis)
criteria.reasoning: Goiter present, but no explicit diagnosis

criteria.text: Age 18-65, for both men and women
Thyroid receptor antibody (TRAb) was positive
Those who have used glucocorticoid or other immunosuppressants for a long time within 3 months before being selected
Ill-controlled hypertension: diastolic blood pressure≥100mmHg, systolic blood pressure ≥160 mmHg
Blood routine: the total number of white blood cells < 3.0×10^9 or neutrophils < 1.5×10^9
Alanine aminotransferase(ALT) or aspartate amino transferase(AST) is greater than 2 times of the normal upper limit
Renal insufficiency: serum creatinine is higher than the upper limit of normal value
Patients with heart failure
Pregnant and lactating women cannot rule out the possibility of pregnancy
criteria.reasoning: No pregnancy information, but not explicitly excluded

trials.title: Thyroidectomy for Graves' Disease or Amiodarone-induced Thyrotoxicosis

criteria.text: Adult patients (≥ 18 years of age)
Patients providing signed informed consent
Children and minors (<18 years)
Pregnant women
Patients unable or unwilling to provide informed consent due to language barriers or intellectual limitations
Patients undergoing concurrent parathyroidectomy for primary or secondary hyperparathyroidism, lymph node clearance, or redo surgery
Surgical procedures performed for other reasons than Graves' disease or AIT
Biochemically confirmed hyperthyroidism or on specific medication for the treatment of Graves' disease or AIT

trials.title: Exploring Immunological Markers Associated With Mental Fatigue in Graves' Disease

criteria.text: Graves' disease with positive TSH-receptor antibodies and thyroid hormones above the upper reference limit at diagnosis
Diagnosis 15 to 60 months ago
Thyroid hormones within normal range without anti thyroid drugs
No thyroid disease
Mental Fatigue Score ≤8
Person unable to follow protocol
criteria.reasoning: No info on ability to follow protocol

criteria.text: Multiple sclerosis, myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome, any other neurological disease
Pregnancy and breast-feeding

trials.rationale: No thyroid disease required, patient has Graves

Patients with diplopia after 3 doses of IVMP
Patients with Graves' Ophthalmopathy who have maintained euthyroidism
Non-pregnant adults (aged 35 years or older)
Individuals with severe hypertension
Individuals with diabetic retinopathy

trials.title: Glucocorticoids Versus Placebo for the Treatment of Acute Exacerbation of Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis

criteria.text: Patient is ≥ 18 years of age
IPF or IPF (likely) diagnosis defined on 2018 international recommendations
criteria.evidence: bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing

criteria.text: Definite or suspected Acute Exacerbation defined by the international working group criteria after exclusion of alternative diagnoses of acute worsening
Previous or concurrent diagnosis of IPF
criteria.evidence: bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing

criteria.text: Acute worsening or development of dyspnea typically < 1-month duration
Identified etiology for acute worsening (i.e.: infectious disease)
Known hypersensitivity to glucocorticoids or to any component of the study treatment
Patient requiring mechanical ventilation or already on mechanical ventilation
Active bacterial, viral, fungal or parasitic infection

trials.rationale: Dyspnea and honeycombing, but uncertain IPF diagnosis

trials.title: Phase II Clinical Study of BC006 in Patients With Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis

criteria.text: Must provide written informed consent form (ICF) indicating understanding of the study and voluntary participation.
Aged ≥40 years at the time of signing the ICF, with no gender restriction.

Diagnosis of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) according to the 2022 American Thoracic Society/European Respiratory Society/Japanese Respiratory Society/Latin American Thoracic Society (ATS/ERS/JRS/ALAT) clinical practice guideline.
criteria.evidence: bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing

criteria.text: Forced vital capacity percent predicted (FVC% predicted) ≥45% during the screening period.
Interstitial lung disease of known etiology (e.g., domestic and occupational environmental exposures, connective tissue disease, drug toxicity, etc.).
criteria.evidence: idiopathic implied by bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing

criteria.text: Other pulmonary diseases considered clinically significant by the investigator (e.g., asthma, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, cavitary or pleural disease, etc.).
Acute exacerbation of IPF within 3 months prior to screening or during the screening period, as judged by the investigator.

Current or suspected malignancy, or history of malignancy within 5 years prior to screening (excluding basal cell carcinoma of the skin, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, superficial bladder cancer, carcinoma in situ of the cervix, or other carcinoma in situ that has received curative treatment with no recurrence).

Active autoimmune disease, or history of autoimmune disease that may relapse (e.g., systemic lupus erythematosus, rheumatoid arthritis, ulcerative colitis, Crohn's disease, autoimmune thyroid disease, multiple sclerosis, vasculitis, glomerulonephritis, etc., including but not limited to these diseases or syndromes).
Pregnant (female patients of childbearing potential confirmed by serum pregnancy test within 7 days prior to first dose of study drug) or lactating female.

trials.rationale: Possible IPF, but uncertain diagnosis and FVC status

trials.title: Intravenous Immunoglobulin for the Treatment of Acute Exacerbations of Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis

criteria.text: Patients ≥ 18 years of age
Patients with IPF diagnosis that fulfils ATS/ERS Consensus Criteria
criteria.evidence: bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing

criteria.text: Patients hospitalised with a definite or suspected AE-IPF diagnosis
Patients with acute worsening or development of dyspnoea typically < 1 month duration
Patients with computed tomography with new bilateral ground-glass opacity and/or consolidation superimposed on a background pattern consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia pattern

criteria.reasoning: No ground-glass opacity or consolidation mentioned

criteria.text: Patients with deterioration not fully explained by cardiac failure or fluid overload
criteria.reasoning: No info on cardiac failure or fluid overload

criteria.text: Patients with acute worsening due to uncontrolled heart failure or pulmonary embolism
criteria.reasoning: No info on heart failure or pulmonary embolism

criteria.text: Patients with known hypersensitivity to corticosteroids, IVIG or any component of the study treatment
Patients with known IgA deficiency (IgA level <7 mg/dL)
Patients with active malignancy or currently receiving cancer treatment, except for basal cell or squamous cell skin cancer or low-risk prostate cancer (T1 or T2a stage with PSA <10 ng/dL)
criteria.reasoning: No cancer or treatment info mentioned

trials.rationale: No new bilateral ground-glass opacity and/or consolidation
